english: dár* (dar), a contrivance for grating radishes, either made of wood, or consisting of a quartz-stone with a crystallized, rough surface.
Will.: 'a woolen blanket or cloth; a sort of deer'; skrai lwá-ba (skra'i lwa ba) Stg. frq. a kind of woolen cloth. The seat of Buddha is often a slab resembling a lwá-ba (lwa ba) Do.
Cs., also la-gór (la gor) Sch., quick, swift, speedy, k̇yod ma _dug ma _dug la-_úr _deñ (khyod ma 'dug ma 'dug la 'ur 'deng) Mil. make haste, go without stopping (on the road).

retribution, punishments overtaking a sinner during this life (cf. lan-čags (lan chags)) C., W.; *la-yóg ṫob yin* (la yog thob yin) that will come home to you! Sch. has la-yogs-pa (la yogs pa) to return, to come back (?).
W. a sort of long-legged and swift-moving centiped, frequent in houses.

C., B. (is said to be pronounced la-lá (la la) in Sp., but Thgy. sometimes accentuates lá-la (la la), according to the metre) some, a few; when put twice: partly - partly, what - what; la-la żig (la la zhig) also as a singular: some body, some one Dzl. VV, 1.
a medicinal herb; in Lh. a Bupleurum.
also dbón-lag (dbon lag), dgón-lag (dgon lag), Sch.: little, not much.

1. resp. ṗyag (phyag), hand, arm, *lág-pa táṅ-če* (lag pa tang ce) W. to shake hands, also to offer one's hand, as a pledge of faith (for C. v. mdzúg-gu (mdzug gu)); lág-pa-nas _jú-ba (lag pa nas 'ju ba) to take, to seize by the hand Dzl.; lag-pai rgyab (lag pa'i rgyab) or bol (bol) the back of the hand; lág-pai mdun (lag pa'i mdun) the palm of the hand Cs.; lág-tu lén-pa (lag tu len pa) to take in hand, to exercise, to practise, sgóm-pa (sgom pa) meditation Mil., tsig-dón (tshig don) to study and practise the import of a word, to live accordingly Mil., metaph.: mtso-lág (mtsho lag) arm of the sea, gulf, bay, mtso-lag-_brél (mtsho lag 'brel) narrow sea, straits; gliṅ-lág (gling lag), yul-lág (yul lag) tongue of land, gliṅ-lag-_brél (gling lag 'brel) isthmus, neck of land C.; fig. for power, authority, mii lág-tu _gró-ba (mi'i lag tu 'gro ba) to get into a person's power, to be at his mercy Thgy., lág-nas _ṗróg-pa (lag nas 'phrog pa) to snatch out of a person's hand, to deliver from another's power Glr. - 2. fore-paw; also paw or foot in gen., e.g. foot of a cock Glr. - 3. symb. num.: 2. Comp. lag-kod (lag kod) bundle, bunch, armful, sheaf of corn Ld. (?). - lag-skór (lag skor) Ld.: hand-mill. - lag-k̇úg (lag khug) pouch, hand-bag Schr. - lag-mgó (lag mgo) 1. lag-mgo tsam (lag mgo tsam) like a fist Glr., or acc. to others: both hands put together in the shape of a globe or ball. 2. a glove with only a thumb, a mitten C. - lag-grám (lag gram) leaning one's head on the hand W. - lag-rgyúgs (lag rgyugs) railing. - lag-rgyún (lag rgyun) accustomed manner, use, habit Cs - lag-ṅár (lag ngar) the fore-arm Wdn. - lág-ča (lag cha) utensils, tools, implements; object carried in the hands, e.g. royal insignia at a festival procession Glr.; also in a more gen. sense, like čá-lag (cha lag), _k̇or-yyóg lág-ča daṅ bcás-pa toṅ żig ('khor yug lag cha dang bcas pa tong zhig) supply servants and things (wanted for the journey)! Glr. - lag-čág (lag chag) a broken hand, a lame hand Cs., Schr. - *lag-čad* (lag chad) W. solemn promise by shaking or joining hands. - lag-rjés (lag rjes) 1. impression, mark, of the hand, of the fingers. 2. a work which immortalizes a person's name, lag-rjes _jóg-pa (lag rjes 'jog pa) to leave such a work behind Glr. - lag-nyá (lag nya), one Lex. has: lag-nyás (lag nyas) = stér-mk̇an-med-par lén-pa (ster mkhan med par len pa) to take what is not given, hence lag-nya (lag nya) prob. a sbst.: a grasp, a snatch. - *lag-nyár* (lag nyar) W. for lag-ṅar (lag ngar). - lag-tig (lag tig) (or dig (dig)?) travelling-bag, pouch Ld. - lag-rtags (lag rtags) 1. resp. ṗyag-rtágs (phyag rtags) q.v., sign or mark made with the hand, as a seal of verification, impressed on a legal document, but often only with the finger dipped in ink. 2. any small object, e.g. a needle, which the deliverer of a letter has to hand over together with the letter; present in general? - lag-stábs (lag stabs) Sch. = lag-
